rootCauseHypothesis:
The test tried to click the text "4", but Playwright reported that a sibling/parent card element with classes like "gradient-border hover-lift fade-in step-glow visible" was intercepting pointer events. This usually means the clickable text is visually present but not actually the topmost interactive element, often due to an overlay, animation layer, or incorrect stacking context/z-index on the hosting side. The element may also be mid-animation and unstable, causing the click target to shift while the test waits. Underlying error: TimeoutError: Locator.click: Timeout 10000ms exceeded. Call log: - waiting for get_by_text("4", exact=True) - locator resolved to 4 - attempting click action - waiting for element to be visible, enabled and stable - element is visible, enabled and stable - scrolling into view if needed - done scrolling -
…
intercepts pointer events - retrying click action - waiting for element to be visible, enabled and stable - element is visible, enabled and stable - scrolling into view if needed - done scrolling -
…
intercepts pointer events - retrying click action - waiting 20ms 2 × waiting for element to be visible, enabled and stable - element is not stable - retrying click action - waiting 100ms - waiting for element to be visible, enabled and stable - element is not stable 17 × retrying click action - waiting 500ms - waiting for element to be visible, enabled and stable - element is visible, enabled and stable - scrolling into view if needed - done scrolling -
…
intercepts pointer events - retrying click action - waiting 500ms

recommendedFixTarget.rationale: Make the numbered step/card clickable at the actual hit target and ensure no overlaying container blocks pointer events. If the number is inside a decorative span, move the click handler to the parent card/button or add pointer-events:none to the overlapping visual layer(s). Also verify any hover/animation layers (e.g. .step-glow/.gradient-border) are not positioned above the clickable element during initial render or transition. If the UI is intended to be clicked by the number, wrap it in a real button with z-index/pointer-events set so it receives the click reliably.
